headlines.title: PHYSICIANS INSPECT FIND CLAREMONT MAN
headlines.subtitle: HUMAN RATTLEBOX IN DRUGGED CONDITION
headlines.fullText: Ellis Whitman Demonstrates Police Establish Identity of E. E. Kutch, Discovered Wandering Around, Unable to Tell Name or His Home.

headlines.title: PLANS TO ENLARGE CLINIC DISCUSSED
headlines.subtitle: Indiscriminate Treatment of Public Is Opposed by Several Physicians; Possible to Erect Large Hospital With Two Charity Wards.
headlines.fullText: Indiscriminate treatment of public is opposed by several physicians; possible to erect large hospital with two charity wards.

headlines.title: FALLS FORTY FEET, STRIKES IRON PIPE
headlines.subtitle: Charles Urban, Bricklayer, Sustains Fractures and Dislocation of Limbs as Result of Fall From Bank Building.
headlines.fullText: Charles Urban, bricklayer, sustains fractures and dislocation of limbs as result of fall from bank building.

headlines.title: FORT WORTH AD MEN ARE LIVEST WIRES
headlines.subtitle: Were There With the Goods at Chicago Meeting of National Executive Committee of Advertising Clubs.
headlines.fullText: Were there with the goods at Chicago meeting of national executive committee of advertising clubs.

headlines.title: EVERYBODY WILL DIG NOVEMBER 5 AND 6
headlines.subtitle: Sweetwater Chamber of Commerce and Fort Worth Are Rustling Volunteers for Pick and Shovel Gang.
headlines.fullText: Sweetwater Chamber of Commerce and Fort Worth are rustling volunteers for pick and shovel gang.

headlines.title: ALL VARIETIES OF FISH FOR THAT NEW LAKE
headlines.subtitle: Claude McCaleb Triumphs in Campaign for Finny Denizens for Minnetonka, Alias West Fork Reservoir.
headlines.fullText: Claude McCaleb triumphs in campaign for finny denizens for Minnetonka, alias West Fork reservoir.

headlines.title: EIGHT CASES DISMISSED IN JUDGE SWAYNE'S COURT
headlines.fullText: Eight cases dismissed in Judge Swayne's court.

headlines.title: POLL TAX ROLLS OPENED AND NINETEEN PAID
headlines.fullText: Poll tax rolls opened and nineteen paid; receipt No. 1, for the Second ward.

headlines.title: TEMPERANCE UNION WILL MEET FRIDAY
headlines.fullText: A parlor meeting of the Woman's Temperance Union will meet Friday.

headlines.title: PLAINTIFFS CHANGED IN RAILROAD SUIT
headlines.fullText: A court order was signed Tuesday (district court at Houston).

headlines.title: FIRE FIGHTERS ENJOY A VERY QUIET MONTH
headlines.fullText: A very quiet month in fire circles.

headlines.title: COLQUITT WILL CALL NEW SPECIAL SESSION TO REPAIR VETO DAMAGE, SAYS TERRELL
headlines.subtitle: House Speaker Is Unsparing in His Criticism of Blue Pencil Demand for New Courts
headlines.fullText: Colquitt will call new special session to repair veto damage, says Terrell. House Speaker is unsparing in his criticism of blue pencil; demand for new courts.

headlines.title: NEW EARTH FORCE HELPS THE SOUTH
headlines.subtitle: Volcanic Fluid Pour in From Everywhere.
headlines.fullText: New earth force helps the South; volcanic fluid pour in from everywhere.

headlines.title: FREAK WEATHER FOR MONTH OF SEPTEMBER
headlines.subtitle: The Hottest, the Coldest, the Wettest, the Brightest, With Very Few Exceptions of Any on Record.
headlines.fullText: Freak weather for month of September: the hottest, the coldest, the wettest, the brightest, with very few exceptions of any on record.

headlines.title: YOUTH PLEADS OWN CASE WITH SUCCESS
headlines.subtitle: A 17-Year-Old Boy Sought to Gain Free Entrance to Dancing Academy on Ladder, but Did Not Trespass.
headlines.fullText: A 17-year-old boy sought to gain free entrance to dancing academy on ladder, but did not trespass. "You are discharged," said Judge.

headlines.title: NEW PASSENGER AGENT FOR THE T. & P. ARRIVES
headlines.fullText: New passenger agent for the T. & P. arrives; Faut Junkin, late of Chicago.
